headline: Apple preparing first foldable iPhone for 2026 launch, supply chain checks suggest
summary: Bank of America’s latest Asia supply chain checks suggest Apple Inc (NASDAQ:AAPL, XETRA:APC) is preparing to introduce its first foldable iPhone in 2026, as the firm reiterated its ‘Buy’ rating while modestly lowering its price objective to $320 from $325. headline: BofA remains bullish on ASML stock after Asia checks
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America remains upbeat on ASML after meetings with investors across Asia, with analyst Didier Scemama stating in a note that the “key message” is that the memory cycle “is likely to remain strong through at least 1H27E.”

headline: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
summary: 1127 ET – Novartis’s share-price strength this year raises the bar for upcoming data on late-stage clinical studies that will be pivotal for the Swiss drugmaker’s outlook, Bank of America analysts say in a research note.  Pipeline failure could hit consensus estimates, but success would help shift investors’ focus away from patent expirations, according to Bank of America.  Among Novartis’s upcoming phase 3 trials, Bank of America is most focused on remibrutinib in relapse-remitting multiple sclerosis and pelacarsen in secondary prevention of cardiovascular events, the analysts say.

headline: U.S. Soccer, Soccer Forward Foundation and Bank of America Launch 'Soccer at Schools' to Bring Soccer to Schools Across the U.S. by 2030

headline: How The Sanmina (SANM) Narrative Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Price Targets And Assumptions
summary: Sanmina’s fair value estimate sits at US$197.50 in the latest analyst work, with the model keeping that headline price target unchanged. Behind that steady figure, research desks are fine tuning their assumptions and using the new targets to separate what looks well supported from what still hinges on Sanmina hitting its marks. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these shifting views and what to watch as the story continues to evolve. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the...
